reviews.review_title: Best single player game on Oculus
reviews.review_description: So far this is my favorite single player game on my headset. It’s creative and immersive. Challenging and feels rewarding when you complete a task or figure out a puzzling area. Definitely check this game out

reviews.review_title: Only good for the first part
reviews.review_description:
The first part of the game (what is considered Budget Cuts 1) is more focused on stealth and that part is pretty cool. However the second part (what is considered Budget Cuts 2) kind of forces you to engage in combat, but the issue is that I find the combat weak compared to VR games released nowadays: it has bad throwing mechanics, and the bow implementation is very finicky (you need to align two separate sights to aim), and because this is the kind of game that has very few arrows available, having janky bow mechanics becomes an issue since every shot counts.
I think it's a good game to get on a sale to experience the first half of the game, but if you want a stealth game with also good combat and bow mechanics then I suggest getting TWD Saints & Sinners instead.

It causes you to lose all your items which some are important to progress in the game. I don't believe there is a way to fix it other than restarting the level the items were on.

reviews.review_title: So much fun!  But bugs ruin it all.
reviews.review_description:
Is it fun?  Absolutely!  One of the best stealth game ever.

Unfortunately, one of the most annoying bug are the checkpoints.  For example, you need to do an action to open a door and once you get on the other side, you decide to quit.  After reloading, you start before opening the door but the action will say "You've already done that", and won't let you open the door again.  You are stuck.  Only solution: Restart the entire level.  It happened to me twice in a short period of time.  A game should entertain you, not make you angry.

Sick of it, I asked Meta for a refund and it was rejected because I played more than 2 hours.  So unfortunate.  Adios Neat Corporation.

The sense of humor, stealth gameplay, and simple, yet artistically pleasing visuals led me to believe I'd have a great time playing Budget Cuts.

It started out ok despite some issues, but then it quickly became a miserable waste of time due to poor throwing mechanics and a serious glitch.

I suspect there's a good game buried underneath the frustrating jank and bugs, but since the developers consider this mess "finished" I'll never know.

I really enjoyed Neat Corporation's earlier Quest game, Garden of the Sea, and I had their upcoming game Crossings on my
wishlist, but after playing Budget Cuts I've removed it. There's a big drop in technical quality between Garden of the Sea and Budget Cuts so my guess is that Crossings will be a mess not just at release, but possibly forever.

The trouble in Budget Cuts starts right from the beginning due to inadequately tested roomscale support.

While they claim to support roomscale and even give you the option to switch off artificial turning if you use your feet, if you try playing in roomscale using natural turning you'll run into glitches.

The tutorial gets stuck right in the beginning because it insists you use artificial turning to proceed, so you have to switch it back on to proceed to the next step.

There's an annoying, immersion breaking blue indicator on the floor that's supposed to indicate which way you're facing, but if you turn with your feet it has no idea where you're facing. There should be an option to switch this indicator off.

Even if you play standing still facing one direction with your feet glued to the ground like a statue and use the thumb stick to turn, you'll still run into issues.

Even something as basic in VR as crouching is glitchy in Budget Cuts.  Since I'm playing in roomscale I thought I could set "auto crouch" to off and use my legs to crouch.  Unfortunately when I first encountered an area that requires crouching, no matter how low I got to the floor I couldn't move forward. I tried switching "auto crouch" on but I still couldn't move forward. It seems like smooth locomotion is disabled in areas where you need to crouch so you have to switch on auto crouching and use teleportation to proceed. This is strange and immersion breaking.

My guess is that Neat Corporation never tested natural turning despite providing the option to turn artificial turning off and never tried crouching with their own legs even though they allow "auto crouch" to be turned off.

If they were honest the store description would say that Budget Cuts only supports "Standing motionless like a statue" instead of "Roomscale, Standing".

The throwing mechanics are truly awful and make it very difficult to hit a target. It's by far the worst throwing I've encountered in VR to date. I never was able to get it to work reliably.

Despite this frustration I had still resigned myself to slogging through the game until I hit the "vanishing inventory" glitch other reviews mentioned.  Restarting a level, (which you'll be doing often since the janky throwing mechanics will make you easy prey for the robots), often results in your inventory disappearing.

It seems like the "Lost and Found" desks scattered throughout the buildings are a band aid to fix the disappearing inventory, but unfortunately they're glitchy also.  If your inventory vanishes the chances are slim that you'll find your missing items in the Lost and Found.

I should have carefully read every review before purchasing. People reported the disappearing inventory glitch over a year ago. The developers haven't updated the game in almost two years. Either they can't fix it or they won't fix it. Either way it's a good reason to avoid their games in the future.

This game isn't just "not worth the money" it's in the "I wouldn't even play it for free" category.

Caveat emptor.

reviews.review_title: A buggy version of a great game.
reviews.review_description:
Back in 2020, I owned both Budget cuts 1 and 2 on PCVR. I always thought this game in concept on Quest would be AMAZING, being untethered and throwing knives without worry about cables.

And this version IS that, don't get me wrong. But there are just so many bugs and weird changes that make it feel unpolished. For example:

-The graphics and rendering distance are pretty bad. I can understand limitations because of the quest 2, but you're telling me that the quest 3 can't render nameplates 5 feet away from me?
-Supervisors are much easier. Their aim is "wonky" compared to their more snappy aim from the original, which makes them less of a threat.
-Tons of bugs. Audio randomly cuts in and out in the train level, ragdolls freak out when moving them, but worst of all is some worker bots no longer wave back at you. Truly the worst bug.

It's a shame because this game is one of the best VR games out there, An OG if you will. But if this is your first and only option, get it anyway. it's still a blast.

Amazing game. Even with cheats enabled it’s still semi difficult. But still lots of fun. It’s got puzzles to figure out. Not regular puzzles but more like riddle kind of puzzles in a way. Either way it’s fun and enjoyable and I highly recommend it.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your kind words! We're stoked that you enjoyed it! :)

reviews.review_description:
Amazing gameplay and levels, the ending was brilliant. The only thing is that there are a few glitches:
After meeting Robert, there’s a taco next to one of the regular robots that can never be eaten and when you try to eat it, it just teleports back to the box. If this was before Robert, it could be a great way to ensure you can always have a taco for him, but since it isn’t, I consider it a glitch. There is also a minor bug where, under the right circumstances, objects and enemies can turn a bit darker, it doesn’t affect anything but I still don’t think it shouldn’t  happen. And finally, when you have a full inventory and you’re carrying an object, but then you go to the next level while still holding the object, it teleports into your inventory giving you temporary access to a larger inventory. This is a beneficial glitch but it’s still on the list. That’s all, I still really love this game and its replayability though!!
If you want a better experience, turn on auto crouch and smooth locomotion. 4 stars for loading times.

Overall, I enjoy this game. The developers really put many efforts to make the story and decoration. The only two things that I have missed to make it 5 stars:
- lack of walking: you can teleport, but you cant walk, except if you have big area to play; it would be great to have one joystick for moving forward
- lack of weapons: although it is enough to pass the game in stealth mode, but if there will be another setting to have more areas with knifes and scissors it would be better for me

I've known about budget cuts FOREVER, so when I saw it on meta+ I was very excited but I ran into a strange problem, it feels like i'm too tall to crouch low enough sometimes,in the first area with the security robot around the cubicles I have to get so low I can't really do much else, I'm 6 foot 6inch tall for reference, there's not a lot in the way of accessibility options for less mobil people it may not be doable. I'm gonna keep trying to find a comfortable way for me cause I'd love to actually play through. Just a heads up for other weirdly tall folk.

I love the game and the premise. It’s a great PCVR game. But- that being said: the one big problem I have with this game is its gimmick, the portals. In the original game you were able to see **everything** through the portal before you entered. Mostly meaning items. The pop-in in this game is in your face all the time and, at least in my opinion, took me out of the experience and made it unplayable for the Quest headsets. That being said, this game is great on PC but I really cannot recommend this port unless this gets fixed.
I'm SO glad that this game is in this month's Meta Quest+ monthly games, or else I would have NEVER found out about this AWESOME game! I DEFINITELY recommended this game to ANYONE that plays on a VR.

developer_response.body: So excited to have you play! Make sure you tell all your friends!

reviews.review_description: Great game! Kept me hooked!

reviews.review_title: Great game, but there’s just one thing
reviews.review_description: The game is good, but I don’t like how you have to manually start a new game just to pick a level. Things from the first and second game like the cost optimizer having a black screen with blue code instead of the normal screen from budget cuts 2 and when it talks about you killing Adam in the factory feels weird, I’m okay with the new screen thing though. Can you fix the glitch with the robots glitching out when you grab them.

"AHHHHHHH, OH GOD HES TRYING TO CRUSH MY LUNGS!!" **PANT**  **PANT** **PANT** **LOUD SCREAMING**

That first part is something that I actually said and did. This game has some really intense moments but also comedy that can make you laugh. (And make you go insane as you try going into the same vent for the sixth time only for you to find out there was a hidden area the whole time and none of that was necessary.)

In all seriousness, 
this game is awesome!

You can teleport and run, solving puzzles, sneaking up on robots, killing, and killing, but being careful where you step. Every story beat has new twists and leaves you wondering, just who is April? What happened to all the humans? 
Who is that handsome, gentle man named A.D.A.M 

All stuff you'll find out in the game, I'm only on the last part of the first one (running for my life in HR)


But so far, the stabbing is fluid. The story is golden, and the best thing I've seen in vr so far, they dont give you the story in unskippable cut scenes (budget cut scenes!) Instead you read stuff and get lore from the robots, papers all over the map, winta, speakers, posters, documents and sometimes even the things chasing you.

Spoiler free story review: without getting into spoilers, the story is about the last human in a place with robots, and you have to fight your way out while being helped by this mysterious caller named winta there's military grade robots and for some reason hundreds of humans were fired and sent to collect their stuff from HR and being sent on their way, never to be heard again.

Action: The action is fluid, and evading robots feels nice, especially the big boy in HR

Stealth: The stealth is fun, not too easy, not too hard, and overall a great balance where you have to search for weapons while avoiding the robots and then kill them the moment you get them.

Reference: theirs so many references to so many series, whether it be posters or lines.

Developers: The devs are hard working and have spent a ton of time making this game perfect for the quest and adding new content!

Comedy: The comedy isn't for everybody, and that's fine. It's not a full comedy game, but it has a few jokes that may make you laugh hysterically  or face palms.

Length: 10-26 hours traditionally

Designs: from the awesome super visor bot designs to the weird potato bots that you see everywhere to A.D.A.M theirs so many good designs

Puzzles: The puzzles will get you thinking, but theirs plenty of hints, whether it be a poster or a dialog line or your magnifying glass telling you important details of documents.

Level design: The level design is intricate and intuitive, every thing was designed to work perfectly even allowing for a run where you don't kill robots (I myself haven't attempted it yet because as said before I'm at the end of budget cuts 1 and ready to begin budget cuts 2)

What it comes with: budget cuts one and two as well as new settings, modifiers, new graphics, the ability to play these two older titles with new graphics and locomotion. You also get new modes like nightmare mode (which I'm terrified of doing)

Movement:There are two ways to move: The translocater, which lets you move by shooting an orb and looking through the portal to see your surroundings in a circle and then pressing the trigger.

Overall, this game is a 10/10 and a must buy in my opinion, I've never been so excited to review a game in my life before this. I've played many games, both story and non story. 
Finally, A.D.A.M. exists, which is enough to try the game in itself, to be honest. A.D.A.M. is a looming threat, and he delivers some of the best lines I've ever heard.

Voice acting: 10/10 next subject

Immersion: The game gets you really immersed in this story to the point where I found myself just exploring aimlessly and admiring everything

Also, the story isn't hidden despite a lot of it being available through things you can miss it's pretty much all told to you with in-game calls and dialog, but some optional story is found around the map. 

I've now finished the game and can safely say it's the best vr experience I've ever had, from the story to the gameplay it's perfect and keeps having me replay to see if I notice any new details or new things to test and I always find something new it's built so well and definitely deserves a sequel or remake of the first two


A.D.A.M. supremacy

reviews.review_title: Good game, but the LOADING SCREEN
reviews.review_description: I like this game, although I’m not in too stealth games I like it of how you can change the game in to a hack and slash game with the cheats, one thing I don’t like is the hellscape your In for half of the game, AKA the loading screen, I mean it does not match the games tone of “kill robots, find people” y’all cooked at the company name screen but not the LOADING SCREEN, COME ON NEAT! (Also music slaps)
was on meta quest+ and the idea is great.should make a part 2

Whilst I really enjoyed the stealth and whimsical humour I had to quit fairly early on. I just couldn't work out where to go. I spent way too much time walking in circles trying to find my way into the vault. It would be nice to have an option for direction when you can't progress. Still recommended.  Maybe it's a me problem.

I sometimes give up on games too easily, so I’ve tried going back and playing the game a few times: I can officially say it’s not me.  The premise is fun, the in-game transportation tool is great (I love the mechanics of it), the levels are well-designed so the sneaking around and avoiding detection is enjoyable; everything about the game seems well thought out and executed until you have to fight security robots, then it is terrible.  
The security robots are WAY TOO DIFFICULT to kill.  Example: I’ve had to use 3 knives to kill one robot (with cheat mode on) and knives aren’t available everywhere.  You have to find them in certain rooms and stock up.  You're also limited to 5 items total, 2 of which are filled by other items required to move forward in the game (e.g.: access cards to different levels, rooms, etc.).
I'm currently on the Quest 2, maybe I'll try again after I upgrade, but I don't think this game is any good on my current system.

THE BEGINNING OF THE GAME IS LITERALLY THE MATRIX! But seriously this game is very fun and scary (I skipped HR and after that cheated till the end of the game btw) and I would highly recommend playing it if you wanna be stealthy and cool. This game also kinda gives me a tiny bit of Hotline Miami vibes cause you die in one hit, cover can be your best friend, you can be a robot killing maniac or be a pacifist for some stages, and all that beautiful oil gore! Oh and if those Supervisors and/or Prosecutors and/or maybe even ADAM are just pissing you off, either turn on cheats (ADAM can still kill you however) or play Robo Recall and be the Robot Doomslayer (or Agent 34) while imagining the rouge robots are supervisors and prosecutors! And like the Administrator from TF2 said: “KILL THE ROBOTS!! BREAK THEIR METAL BONES!!”

MAJOR SPOILERS. This is NOT an over all review of the game just something I wanted to say. The first time I beat the game I didn’t kill Adam I was able to grab a supervisor head with a well timed stoping of the scraper and then when I saw his head on a mantle at the bar I just thought “the other bot’s killed him” and just thought it was disappointing to not see his death .But I just beat it again and realized that the light bulb maker was there for a reason and it was REALLY satisfying to see him die. The fact that you can’t even stun him with things like coffee mugs and thing like that also the  fact the game makes sure of it by removing all your items and even if you cheat and use the infinite staby crystal’s it just BOUNCES RIGHT OFF HIM. In Another word’s good job Neat corporation for making robots scary

i’m surprised this has so many good reviews. did they even try on the graphics ?? or was this just a money grab. the price does not match the quality.

developer_response.body:
we did try ;_; 
but this game definitely had more of a focus on putting in lots of gameplay moments and lots of levels, but not a huge focus on high polycount and highly detailed textures. we went for a more stylized approach, so that it would run smoothly on most hardware. otherwise the levels would have had to be a bout 1/4th their current size, which just wasn't as much fun to play.
